destinations.evidence: The regular transaction execution path consults parent-state AsyncJobTracker-backed sender-lock logic and skips both true results and registry read errors.
The helper implements the same allow/skip cases as the invariant: no jobs allow, settled or expired jobs allow, existing-job fulfillment allows, otherwise sender-locked transactions skip.
The verifier uses the same contract-backed sender job and existing-job fulfillment semantics, which anchors builder/verifier parity.

equivalence_argument: The payload builder and verifier both use AsyncJobTracker parent/contract state via ritual-async-registry. The builder skips only when the helper says the sender is locked and the tx is not an existing-job fulfillment, and it skips on read errors to avoid producing blocks the verifier would reject.
rationale: The commit message says stale in-memory sender lock state caused verifier rejection, and the primary diff invokes ritual_async_registry::should_skip_sender_locked_tx for each regular transaction using a StateProviderDatabase over parent state. This result keeps the candidate as a distinct refined invariant because the bounded assignment packet does not contain a newer candidate that clearly supersedes it. Implementation-detail caveat: The exact helper name and database wrapper are implementation details; the intended behavior is builder/verifier agreement on contract-backed sender locks.
statement: During regular transaction processing, the payload builder must call should_skip_sender_locked_tx against parent-state AsyncJobTracker data and skip the transaction whenever the helper returns true.
suggested_falsification_tests: Populate parent-state AsyncJobTracker so a sender has an unsettled async job and provide a normal non-fulfillment transaction from that sender in the pool. Assert payload building does not include that transaction. Then mark all sender jobs settled or make the transaction a fulfillment and assert the sender-lock check no longer excludes it.
summary: Regular transaction processing skips contract-locked senders in the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Populate parent-state AsyncJobTracker so a sender has an unsettled async job and provide a normal non-fulfillment transaction from that sender in the pool. Assert payload building does not include that transaction. Then mark all sender jobs settled or make the transaction a fulfillment and assert the sender-lock check no longer excludes it.
title: Builder skips contract-locked senders
